title: Payback Period Rule: If a firm has a cash outflow of 75000 and...
summary: Calculate the payback period by dividing the initial investment by annual cash inflows.
prompt: If a firm has a cash outflow of $75,000 and cash inflows of $30,000 for 4 years, what is the payback period?
solution: Payback Period = Initial Investment / Annual Cash Flow. Thus, $75,000 / $30,000 = 2.5 years, indicating it will take approximately 3 years to recover the investment.
